---
title: "Explorationbins"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

# Load required packages
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,    # Data manipulation and plotting
  lme4,         # Mixed effects models
  lmerTest,     # P-values for mixed models
  arrow,        # Parquet file I/O
  stringi,      # String operations
  lubridate,    # Date/time handling
  zoo,          # Rolling statistics
  patchwork,    # Combine plots
  cowplot,      # Additional plot utilities
  afex,          # ANOVA
  here,          # File path management
  emmeans      # Estimated marginal means

)

# Set project root
PROJECT_ROOT <- here()
setwd(PROJECT_ROOT)

# Set global theme
theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 14))

# Define output directories
GRAPHS_DIR <- file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "analysis", "figures")
TABLES_DIR <- file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "analysis", "tables")

# Create output directories if they don't exist
dir.create(GRAPHS_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(TABLES_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
```

# 1. Data Loading and Preprocessing

## 1.1 Load Data

```{r load-data}

# Load Field Data
setwd(PROJECT_ROOT)
df_res_field <- read_parquet("data/TEXT/processed/semantic_exploration_binned.parquet") %>%
  mutate(
    datatype = "Field"
  )


```
## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
df_res_field %>%
  filter(agent != 'interleaved') %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = k, y = distance, color = agent)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Distance",
    x = "Bin size",
    y = "Distance"
  ) +
  ylim(0.05, 0.3)
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
lm <- df_res_field %>%
  filter(agent != "interleaved") %>%
  mutate(
    # flatten list-column to character
    conversation_id = map_chr(conversation_id, as.character),
    # and treat it as a grouping factor
    conversation_id = factor(conversation_id)
  )

lm_field <- lmer(distance ~ k * agent + (1 | conversation_id), data = lm)

summary(lm_field)
```
